one_liner.value: Lark (Lark Suite) is an all-in-one AI-powered enterprise collaboration platform combining messaging, docs, meetings, calendars, project management, and workflow automation, with an extensive Open API (2500+ APIs) and developer ecosystem.

methods.notes: Lark Open Platform uses App ID + App Secret as API key credentials to obtain access tokens. OAuth 2.0 is used for user-scoped authorization (user_access_token). The primary auth flow for server-to-server API calls is API-key-based (App ID + App Secret → tenant_access_token). For user-scoped operations, OAuth 2.0 is required.

access.notes: Any developer can sign up at open.larksuite.com and create a custom app to get App ID and App Secret for free. The free Starter plan is available with no credit card. Basic API capabilities (authentication, contacts, event subscription) are free regardless of plan. Some advanced APIs may require quota packages or higher-tier plans, but basic agent integration is possible on the free tier.

summary.value: Lark Open Platform provides a documented public REST API with 2500+ endpoints covering Contacts, Messenger, Docs, Base (spreadsheet/database), Sheets, Wiki, Approval, Calendar, Video Conferencing, Tasks, Email, AI, and more. The API follows RESTful style. Also supports event subscriptions (webhooks) and callback mechanisms. Official SDKs available for Python (lark-oapi), Node.js, Go, Java. Comprehensive API documentation at open.larksuite.com with API Explorer.

agent_interface.notes: Lark/Feishu provides an official open-source MCP server ('lark-openapi-mcp') on GitHub (MIT license) with 700+ stars. It supports both app-identity (tenant_access_token) and user-identity OAuth flows. Additionally, a community MCP server exists. Lark also has an official SDK (lark-oapi for Python) and an official CLI for AI agents.

rationale.value: Lark is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented, broad REST API (2500+ endpoints), an official open-source MCP server maintained by the Lark team, standard OAuth 2.0 and API-key auth, free self-serve developer credentials (no paid plan required for basic access), and official SDKs in multiple languages. The only minor friction is that some advanced APIs require quota packages, but core messaging, contacts, docs, and calendar APIs are available on the free tier.

s7.quote: Unlimited: Core features including Authentication and Authorization, Event Subscription, and Contacts are free to use, regardless of your Lark plan. Basic: This covers most Lark features. Usage is tallied... Advanced: Pricing for these APIs is usage-based.

s10.quote: This is the Feishu/Lark official OpenAPI MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool... Complete Feishu/Lark API Toolkit: Encapsulates almost all Feishu/Lark API interfaces
s10.title: Feishu/Lark OpenAPI MCP Server | Awesome MCP Servers

limitations: The exact API usage limits on the free Starter plan are not explicitly documented on the pricing page, but the API list page indicates core APIs are free regardless of plan.
Composio does not appear to have a dedicated Lark toolkit pre-built, but the official MCP server and REST API can be used directly.
Some sensitive data APIs (e.g., Contacts, Lark HR) require additional data permission configuration and review.
